examples.title: Basic Union Declaration and Usage
examples.description: Demonstrates basic union declaration, memory allocation, and accessing members
examples.explanation: This program shows that all union members share the same memory. When one member is assigned, it corrupts the values of other members because they all point to the same memory location. The size is 4 bytes (largest member - int/float).

examples.title: Extracting Bytes from Integer Using Union
examples.description: Uses union to break an integer into its individual bytes - useful for understanding byte-level storage
examples.explanation: The union allows viewing the same 4 bytes as either a complete integer or as an array of 4 bytes. On little-endian systems, the least significant byte appears first (0x78 at index 0). This technique is used in low-level programming for serialization and network protocols.

examples.title: Tagged Union for Employee Records
examples.description: Implements tagged union pattern to safely store different employee ID types
examples.explanation: This demonstrates the tagged union pattern where an enum tracks which union member is active. The type field ensures we read the correct interpretation of the shared memory. Memory saved compared to having two separate fields: 20 bytes for alphanumeric instead of 24 (int + 20 char array).
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for string operations, O(1) otherwise

examples.title: Union within Structure - Anonymous Union Demo
examples.description: Shows how unions can be nested within structures with anonymous union for cleaner syntax
examples.explanation: This demonstrates anonymous unions inside structures. Members of the anonymous union (intValue, floatValue, text) can be accessed directly without the union name. The struct size is 4 (type) + 50 (largest union member) = 54 bytes (plus padding). This pattern is useful for implementing message passing systems with different payload types.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for string operations, O(1) otherwise
